name: Sigil charging ob3ect: chaos magic sigilization where intention is encoded into an abstract glyph, charged with gnosis, then forgotten. FSPLIT: the statement of desire is decomposed into its constituent letters, stripped of repeating letters, and the remaining letters are abstracted into a graphic sigil — the intention splits into pure symbolic essence. FFUSE: gnostic charging (orgasm, meditation, pain, or exhaustion) fires the sigil, reconstituting the original intention into reality. VINIT: unformed desire. TANCH: the completed sigil. AFWD: charging with gnosis. AREV: forgetting (the sigil must be forgotten to fire). ENGAGR: the sigil is simultaneously a meaningless squiggle and a container of potent will.

phase_0.domain_name: Sigil charging ob3ect: chaos magic sigilization where intention is encoded into an abstract glyph, charged with gnosis, then forgotten. FSPLIT: the statement of desire is decomposed into its constituent letters, stripped of repeating letters, and the remaining letters are abstracted into a graphic sigil — the intention splits into pure symbolic essence. AFWD: charging with gnosis. AREV: forgetting (the sigil must be forgotten to fire). ENGAGR: the sigil is simultaneously a meaningless squiggle and a container of potent will.
phase_0.boundary_condition: the completed sigil that contains the intention

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any intention is named

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains the whole system of intention

AFWD.justification: forward morphism that fires the sigil toward manifestation

AREV.justification: reverse/descent that allows the sigil to operate unconsciously

CLINK.chosen_element: statement of desire → letter decomposition → abstraction
CLINK.justification: sequential chaining of morphisms from desire to glyph

IMSCRIB.justification: identity: the glyph is itself, a container of its own meaning

FSPLIT.justification: the statement of desire splits into its constituent letters, stripped of repeats, abstracted into a graphic sigil

FFUSE.justification: orgasm, meditation, pain, or exhaustion reconstitutes the original intention into reality

EVALT.justification: affirmative state where the desire is realized

EVALF.justification: negative state where the intention remains unrealized

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the sigil as both meaningless squiggle and potent will

IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible record of the intention in the universe

phase_3.void_description: unformed desire before any sigil work
phase_3.false_description: sigil fails to fire, intention unrealized
phase_3.both_description: sigil is simultaneously meaningless and potent

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the magician recognizes the self as the source of will
Step 2 AREV:   the magician descends into gnostic state (meditation, exhaustion, etc.)
Step 3 FSPLIT: the desire statement is decomposed into letters, stripped, abstracted into a sigil
Step 4 AFWD:   the sigil is charged with gnosis (orgasm, pain, etc.)
Step 5 FFUSE:  the charged sigil fires, reconstituting the intention into reality
Step 6 CLINK:  the magician composes the sigil's effect with daily life
Step 7 IFIX:   the sigil is drawn and charged, permanently recorded
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the magician recognizes the sigil as a part of the self

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the magician's will that translates desire into sigil
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: gnostic energy transfer between sigil and universe
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the sigil itself as a stored glyph
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the timing of gnostic charging and forgetting
phase_5.alfs_store: the canonical sigil magic tradition (e.g., Spare's method)

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle conserves the intention's informational content
